title: GBP/USD slips as Iran hope fades and Dollar firms
body: GBP/USD edged slightly lower to around 1.3550 on Thursday, lower around 0.3% after the pair touched a session high near 1.3645 before turning back down.

title: AUD/USD slips from multi-year highs as Aussie trade slumps and Dollar bounces
body: AUD/USD edged about 0.2% lower on Thursday to around 0.7205, easing back from a multi-year peak just shy of 0.7280 reached earlier in the session.

title: USD/JPY rebounds from lows as Iran ceasefire optimism wavers ahead of NFP
body: USD/JPY recovered close to 156.90 on Thursday, edging up roughly 0.4% from Wednesday's slide to 155.04, the lowest level since early February.

title: Mexican Peso falls as Banxico cuts rates, Hormuz tensions rise
body: The Mexican Peso erases some of its earlier gains and drops some 0.13% as the USD/MXN pair advances after the Bank of Mexico (Banxico) cut rates and warned that the easing cycle has ended. The exotic pair trades at 17.27 after testing a low of 17.19.

title: NZD/USD struggles as fragile US-Iran peace talks keep markets cautious
body: The NZD/USD pair is trading with a softer tone near the 0.5940 region on Friday, pressured by cautious market sentiment as investors continue tp react to rapidly changing headlines surrounding the potential peace agreement between the United States (US) and Iran.

title: USD/IDR: Overbought conditions and support levels – OCBC
body: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong describe USD/IDR as easing from overbought territory as hopes of a US–Iran deal support sentiment. Bank Indonesia’s (BI) tighter rules on cash FX purchases and comments that the Rupiah (IDR) is undervalued back stabilization efforts.

title: South Korea: Modest tightening prospects – DBS
body: DBS Group Research economist Ma Tieying expects the Bank of Korea (BoK) to keep its base rate at 2.50% at the May 28 meeting, but now projects a single 25 bps hike in 3Q 2026 to 2.75%.

title: Forex Today: US Dollar holds firm amid fragile US-Iran peace talks
body: The US Dollar Index (DXY) is trading with a softer tone near the 98.10 area as markets continue reacting to rapidly shifting headlines surrounding the potential peace agreement between the United States (US) and Iran.

title: Gold holds above $4,700 as Hormuz tensions revive USD demand
body: Gold (XAU/USD) edges higher on Thursday during the North American session amid heightened tensions in the Middle East due to rumors that the US is looking to restart Project Freedom. At the time of writing, XAU/USD trades at $4,705 after reaching a daily high of $4,764.

title: Banxico cuts rates 25 bps to 6.50% as expected
body: Banco de Mexico (Banxico) reduced borrowing costs by 25 basis points on Thursday, as expected by market participants, from 6.75% to 6.50%, even though the central bank revealed that the “balance of risks for the trajectory of inflation within the forecast horizon remains biased to the upside.”

title: Thailand: Cost-push spike, steady BoT rate – UOB
body: UOB economists Enrico Tanuwidjaja and Sathit Talaengsatya argue that Thailand’s sharp April Consumer Price Index (CPI) jump is driven mainly by energy and selective food pass-through rather than broad demand-led reflation.

title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD tests upper Bollinger Band while momentum remains subdued
body: Silver (XAG/USD) trims part of its earlier gains on Thursday as geopolitical headlines from the Middle East continue to stir volatility across financial markets.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD struggles to recover its long-term momentum
body: Spot Gold advances for a third consecutive day, hitting an intraday high of $4,765 before retreating some.

title: NOK/SEK: Is the Nordic divergence only getting started?
body: The NOK/SEK pair highlights an increasingly clear divergence between two neighboring economies that are often seen as similar, but are exposed to very different drivers. The current context favors the Norwegian Krone (NOK) over its Swedish counterpart, but for how long?

title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Bullish near multi-year top amid Iran peace deal hopes, hawkish RBA
body: The AUD/USD pair attracts some buyers for the third consecutive day on Thursday and remains within striking distance of its highest level since June 2022, around the 0.7275-0.7280 region, which it touched the previous day.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD confirms a falling wedge breakout on US-Iran peace deal optimism
body: Gold is gathering pace early Thursday, after having hit one-week highs above $4,700 on Tuesday. Markets remain expectant of a potential US-Iran peace deal being reached, supporting the renewed uptrend in Gold.

title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: The 0.7300 hurdle comes next
body: The Aussie Dollar is attempting to break above the ongoing multi-day consolidative phase, with AUD/USD reaching fresh yearly peaks near the 0.7300 barrier. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s cautious stance.

title: XAG/USD Price analysis: Silver recovers its spark, next hurdle at $80
body: Silver edged higher on Wednesday, as optimism put a halt to US Dollar (USD) demand. Market players welcomed headlines indicating that the United States (US) and Iran were closing in on a deal to end the Middle East conflict.

title: The Fed Warsh inherits: Divided and with its independence in doubt
body: The Federal Reserve is the central bank of the world’s most powerful economy, and prides itself on its independence. But is it? Or, better said, will it continue to be under Kevin Warsh?

title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Seems vulnerable amid suspected JPY intervention, weaker USD
body: The USD/JPY pair attracts heavy intraday selling on Wednesday and dives to the 155.00 psychological mark, or the lowest level since February 24, though it lacks follow-through.

title: TOWIE star dies aged 35
body: The mother of reality star Jake Hall's daughter has spoken of their "devastating loss" after the TV star died at the age of 35.

title: All restrictions on Belarusian athletes lifted by Olympic bosses
body: The International Olympic Committee has lifted all restrictions on Belarusian athletes.

title: Three women with alleged links to ISIS set to be charged after arriving in Australia
body: Three women with alleged links to the Islamic State group's former so-called caliphate in Syria are set to face charges, including slavery and terror offences, Australian police have said.

title: 'Unbelievably lucky' no one harmed after megatsunami
body: A tsunami at a popular tourist spot in Alaska was the second highest ever recorded and it was "unbelievably lucky" no one was hurt, researchers have said.

title: EU waters down landmark AI regulation amid industry pressure
body: Overnight, EU lawmakers agreed to water down their AI legislation that was world-leading when it launched in 2024.
